threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still check your phone for train times instead of just trusting the Tube delays—welcome to the game of patience, mate.
Last night on the 59, a woman serenaded us with a haunting cover of 'Creep'. By the time we hit Streatham, even the driver was clapping.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a kid selling homemade lemonade at the corner of Hermannstraße while a cat lounged on a bicycle nearby, I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab place where the chef argues with the delivery driver over the best way to wrap it. It's an art form here.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local when they know to avoid Dolores Park on sunny weekends—it's a total zoo with people blaring music and taking selfies.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just watched a guy sprinting alongside the Muni bus, yelling for the driver to stop. The bus? Stopped for a dog on the tracks. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is just for Gaudí. They miss the real charm in the tiny squares where locals sip vermut and chat for hours.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their bewilderment at the uneven cobblestones; they still think they can walk without tripping.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a 'tube' and not a 'London Underground' like everyone else around here. You’ll get there, just give it time.
Last night on the 35, heard a guy start his own pub quiz. He was asking about obscure 90s TV shows and everyone was chiming in. Pure Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids breakdancing on the corner, while an elderly man sold homemade jam from his bicycle. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a döner at 3 AM, hear a spontaneous concert outside a club, and find an artist painting murals on the sidewalk—all at once.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local if they can identify every taco truck by the owner’s name and their go-to order, not just the menu.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Yesterday, a dog held up the entire Muni line at Civic Center. Only in SF would a pup create such chaos—seems like every transit adventure here has a twist.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: They think all the buildings are Gaudí's. Just stroll down Carrer de Perill for some beautiful examples of modernisme that go unnoticed.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their constant snapping of photos at Plaça del Sol, as if every angle is a hidden gem.
